     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# TuringOS v4 Harness Validation — Zero API Cost
     3	# Tests hooks, rule engine, case library, and structure by direct simulation.
     4	# Usage: bash tests/harness_validation.sh
     5	
     6	set -uo pipefail
     7	
     8	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
     9	PASS=0
    10	FAIL=0
    11	TOTAL=0
    12	
    13	pass() { ((PASS++)); ((TOTAL++)); printf "  \033[32m[PASS]\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
    14	fail() { ((FAIL++)); ((TOTAL++)); printf "  \033[31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s → %s\n" "$1" "$2"; }
    15	
    16	echo "=========================================="
    17	echo " TuringOS v4 Harness Validation Suite"
    18	echo " Project: $ROOT"
    19	echo "=========================================="
    20	
    21	# ============================================================
    22	echo ""
    23	echo "--- 1. Structure Checks ---"
    24	# ============================================================
    25	
    26	# T-001: CLAUDE.md exists and < 50 lines
    27	LINES=$(wc -l < "$ROOT/CLAUDE.md")
    28	[ "$LINES" -lt 50 ] && pass "T-001: CLAUDE.md = ${LINES} lines (< 50)" || fail "T-001: CLAUDE.md too long" "${LINES} lines"
    29	
    30	# T-002: constitution.md exists
    31	[ -f "$ROOT/constitution.md" ] && pass "T-002: constitution.md exists" || fail "T-002: constitution.md missing" ""
    32	
    33	# T-003: constitution.md is the sole alignment document
    34	[ -f "$ROOT/constitution.md" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/handover/bible.md" ] && pass "T-003: constitution.md is sole alignment doc" || fail "T-003: constitution.md missing or bible.md still exists" ""
    35	
    36	# T-004: 3 hooks exist and are executable
    37	HOOK_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/.claude/hooks" -name "*.sh" -executable | wc -l)
    38	[ "$HOOK_COUNT" -eq 3 ] && pass "T-004: 3 executable hooks" || fail "T-004: Expected 3 hooks" "found $HOOK_COUNT"
    39	
    40	# T-005: 3 agents defined
    41	AGENT_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/.claude/agents" -name "*.md" | wc -l)
    42	[ "$AGENT_COUNT" -eq 3 ] && pass "T-005: 3 agent definitions" || fail "T-005: Expected 3 agents" "found $AGENT_COUNT"
    43	
    44	# T-006: 6 skills defined
    45	SKILL_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/.claude/skills" -name "SKILL.md" | wc -l)
    46	[ "$SKILL_COUNT" -eq 6 ] && pass "T-006: 6 skill definitions" || fail "T-006: Expected 6 skills" "found $SKILL_COUNT"
    47	
    48	# T-007: 10 active rules
    49	RULE_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/rules/active" -name "*.yaml" | wc -l)
    50	[ "$RULE_COUNT" -eq 10 ] && pass "T-007: 10 active rules" || fail "T-007: Expected 10 rules" "found $RULE_COUNT"
    51	
    52	# T-008: 5 docs
    53	DOC_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/docs" -name "*.md" | wc -l)
    54	[ "$DOC_COUNT" -eq 5 ] && pass "T-008: 5 docs files" || fail "T-008: Expected 5 docs" "found $DOC_COUNT"
    55	
    56	# T-009: 9 incidents migrated
    57	INCIDENT_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/incidents" -maxdepth 1 -type d -name "V-*" | wc -l)
    58	[ "$INCIDENT_COUNT" -eq 9 ] && pass "T-009: 9 incidents migrated" || fail "T-009: Expected 9 incidents" "found $INCIDENT_COUNT"
    59	
    60	# T-010: 35 cases in library
    61	CASE_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/cases" -name "C-*.yaml" | wc -l)
    62	[ "$CASE_COUNT" -eq 35 ] && pass "T-010: 35 cases in library" || fail "T-010: Expected 35 cases" "found $CASE_COUNT"
    63	
    64	# T-011: settings.json valid JSON
    65	python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$ROOT/.claude/settings.json'))" 2>/dev/null \
    66	  && pass "T-011: settings.json valid JSON" || fail "T-011: settings.json invalid" "parse error"
    67	
    68	# T-012: No GAIA OVERRIDE in agents
    69	if grep -rq "GAIA" "$ROOT/.claude/agents/" 2>/dev/null; then
    70	  fail "T-012: GAIA remnants found" "$(grep -rl GAIA "$ROOT/.claude/agents/")"
    71	else
    72	  pass "T-012: No GAIA Override remnants"
    73	fi
    74	
    75	# T-013: CLAUDE.md references cases/
    76	grep -q "cases" "$ROOT/CLAUDE.md" && pass "T-013: CLAUDE.md references case library" || fail "T-013: CLAUDE.md missing cases/ ref" ""
    77	
    78	# T-014: handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md references v4
    79	grep -q "v4" "$ROOT/handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md" && pass "T-014: LATEST.md is v4" || fail "T-014: LATEST.md not v4" ""
    80	
    81	# ============================================================
    82	echo ""
    83	echo "--- 2. Rule Engine (direct Python tests) ---"
    84	# ============================================================
    85	
    86	ENGINE="$ROOT/rules/engine.py"
    87	RULES="$ROOT/rules/active"
    88	TRACES="/tmp/v4_harness_test_traces"
    89	rm -rf "$TRACES"
    90	
    91	# T-015: engine.py runs without error on clean input
    92	OUTPUT=$(echo "normal safe code" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/bus.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
    93	EC=$?
    94	[ $EC -eq 0 ] && pass "T-015: engine.py clean input → exit 0" || fail "T-015: Clean input should pass" "exit $EC: $OUTPUT"
    95	
    96	# T-016: R-001 blocks domain leak in kernel.rs
    97	OUTPUT=$(echo "let x = lean_tactic_theorem" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/kernel.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
    98	EC=$?
    99	[ $EC -eq 2 ] && pass "T-016: R-001 blocks kernel domain leak (exit 2)" || fail "T-016: Should block domain strings" "exit $EC"
   100	
   101	# T-017: R-002 blocks coin minting
   102	OUTPUT=$(echo "fund_agent(agent, 1000)" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/bus.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
   103	EC=$?
   104	[ $EC -eq 2 ] && pass "T-017: R-002 blocks fund_agent (exit 2)" || fail "T-017: Should block fund_agent" "exit $EC"
   105	
   106	# T-018: R-003 blocks WAL deletion pattern
   107	OUTPUT=$(echo "std::fs::remove_file(wal_path)" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/wal.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
   108	EC=$?
   109	# R-003 checks for wal deletion — may or may not match depending on glob

     1	// PPUT-CCL Phase B B7 — Trust Root + Boot freeze (PREREG § 1.8 + § 7).
     2	//
     3	// Constitutional anchor: FC3-S3 `readonly` subgraph (constitution.md
     4	// line 670, system-level flowchart). The constitutional readonly base
     5	// is {constitution-as-ground-truth, logs-archive-as-ground-truth}; B7
     6	// extends this base per PREREG § 1.8 to also cover the case-law glob,
     7	// pre-registration spec, heldout splits, and the PPUT accounting layer.
     8	// TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC3-N34 was 📅 Phase 11+ ("FS-level readonly
     9	// check at init") — B7 implements it via SHA-256 manifest verification.
    10	// See `handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md`.
    11	//
    12	// At Boot we hash every tracked file and compare against the
    13	// `[trust_root]` manifest in `genesis_payload.toml`. Any mismatch =>
    14	// `TrustRootError::Tampered { .. }`. `src/main.rs` panics with
    15	// `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED`.
    16	//
    17	// Manifest derivation (Phase B7, independently re-derived from PREREG
    18	// § 1.8 + B2-B4 mid-term audit recommendation + B6 prompt_guard add):
    19	// see header comment in `genesis_payload.toml`.
    20	//
    21	// TOML parsing is hand-rolled (~30 LOC). The manifest format is flat:
    22	// section header + `"path" = "hash"` lines. Adding a `toml` crate
    23	// dependency would drag in ~5 transitive crates for what we can do
    24	// in-line; compression principle (CLAUDE.md "反奥利奥架构") wins.
    25	
    26	use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
    27	use std::fs;
    28	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    29	
    30	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: failure variants of the readonly-guard verification.
    31	/// Constitutional role = the diagnostic surface that distinguishes
    32	/// `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED` (real readonly violation) from `GenesisRead` /
    33	/// `GenesisParse` (manifest itself unreadable, also a violation but a
    34	/// different fix path).
    35	#[derive(Debug)]
    36	pub enum TrustRootError {
    37	    GenesisRead(std::io::Error),
    38	    GenesisParse(String),
    39	    SectionMissing(&'static str),
    40	    FileRead { path: PathBuf, err: std::io::Error },
    41	    Tampered { path: PathBuf, expected: String, actual: String },
    42	}
    43	
    44	impl std::fmt::Display for TrustRootError {
    45	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    46	        match self {
    47	            Self::GenesisRead(e) => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: cannot read genesis_payload.toml: {e}"),
    48	            Self::GenesisParse(s) => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: genesis_payload.toml parse error: {s}"),
    49	            Self::SectionMissing(s) => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: genesis_payload.toml missing section [{s}]"),
    50	            Self::FileRead { path, err } => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: cannot read tracked file {}: {err}", path.display()),
    51	            Self::Tampered { path, expected, actual } => write!(
    52	                f,
    53	                "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: {} hash mismatch (expected {}, actual {})",
    54	                path.display(), expected, actual
    55	            ),
    56	        }
    57	    }
    58	}
    59	
    60	impl std::error::Error for TrustRootError {}
    61	
    62	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: implementation of the constitutional `readonly`
    63	/// subgraph (constitution.md FC3, system-level flowchart). Verifies every
    64	/// tracked file's SHA-256 against the `genesis_payload.toml [trust_root]`
    65	/// manifest at Boot. Mismatch => Boot abort; the readonly guarantee that
    66	/// the constitution requires of {constitution, logs} (extended per PREREG
    67	/// § 1.8 to the full PPUT-accounting base) is enforced here.
    68	///
    69	/// `repo_root` is the directory containing `genesis_payload.toml` (typically
    70	/// the workspace root). Paths in the manifest are interpreted relative to it.
    71	pub fn verify_trust_root(repo_root: &Path) -> Result<(), TrustRootError> {
    72	    let genesis_path = repo_root.join("genesis_payload.toml");
    73	    let genesis_text = fs::read_to_string(&genesis_path).map_err(TrustRootError::GenesisRead)?;
    74	    let manifest = parse_trust_root_section(&genesis_text)?;
    75	    if !has_section(&genesis_text, "pput_accounting_0") {
    76	        return Err(TrustRootError::SectionMissing("pput_accounting_0"));
    77	    }
    78	    for (rel_path, expected) in &manifest {
    79	        let full = repo_root.join(rel_path);
    80	        let bytes = fs::read(&full).map_err(|err| TrustRootError::FileRead {
    81	            path: full.clone(),
    82	            err,
    83	        })?;
    84	        let actual = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(&bytes));
    85	        if actual != *expected {
    86	            return Err(TrustRootError::Tampered {
    87	                path: full,
    88	                expected: expected.clone(),
    89	                actual,
    90	            });
    91	        }
    92	    }
    93	    Ok(())
    94	}
    95	
    96	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: helper for `verify_trust_root` — exposed because
    97	/// the trust_root_immutability conformance battery (Phase B7) reads the
    98	/// manifest directly to assert it includes the audit-recommended PPUT
    99	/// accounting layer.
   100	///
   101	/// Parses the `[trust_root]` section of `genesis_payload.toml` into ordered
   102	/// `(path, sha256)` pairs. Hand-rolled — accepts the narrow subset we emit
   103	/// (quoted-key = quoted-value, comments, blank lines).
   104	pub fn parse_trust_root_section(text: &str) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, TrustRootError> {
   105	    let mut in_section = false;
   106	    let mut entries = Vec::new();
   107	    for (lineno, raw) in text.lines().enumerate() {
   108	        let line = strip_comment(raw).trim();
   109	        if line.is_empty() {
   110	            continue;
   111	        }
   112	        if let Some(header) = line.strip_prefix('[').and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix(']')) {
   113	            in_section = header.trim() == "trust_root";
   114	            continue;
   115	        }
   116	        if !in_section {
   117	            continue;
   118	        }
   119	        let (key, value) = line.split_once('=').ok_or_else(|| {
   120	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("line {}: missing '=' in [trust_root]", lineno + 1))
   121	        })?;
   122	        let key = unquote(key.trim()).ok_or_else(|| {
   123	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("line {}: key not quoted", lineno + 1))
   124	        })?;
   125	        let value = unquote(value.trim()).ok_or_else(|| {
   126	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("line {}: value not quoted", lineno + 1))
   127	        })?;
   128	        entries.push((key.to_string(), value.to_string()));
   129	    }
   130	    if entries.is_empty() {
   131	        return Err(TrustRootError::SectionMissing("trust_root"));
   132	    }
   133	    Ok(entries)
   134	}
   135	
   136	fn has_section(text: &str, name: &str) -> bool {
   137	    text.lines().any(|raw| {
   138	        let line = strip_comment(raw).trim();
   139	        line
   140	            .strip_prefix('[')
   141	            .and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix(']'))
   142	            .map(|h| h.trim() == name)
   143	            .unwrap_or(false)
   144	    })
   145	}
   146	
   147	fn strip_comment(line: &str) -> &str {
   148	    let mut in_string = false;
   149	    for (i, c) in line.char_indices() {
   150	        match c {
   151	            '"' => in_string = !in_string,
   152	            '#' if !in_string => return &line[..i],
   153	            _ => {}
   154	        }
   155	    }
   156	    line
   157	}
   158	
   159	fn unquote(s: &str) -> Option<&str> {
   160	    s.strip_prefix('"').and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix('"'))
   161	}
   162	
   163	fn hex_lower(bytes: &[u8]) -> String {
   164	    use std::fmt::Write;
   165	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(bytes.len() * 2);
   166	    for b in bytes {
   167	        write!(out, "{b:02x}").unwrap();
   168	    }
   169	    out
   170	}
   171

handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:5:**Type**: alignment observation, no constitution change requested
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:6:**Per CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard"**: constitution.md hygiene observations登记到 handover/alignment/OBS_*.md, 不改宪法.
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:14:This panic is **not** a FC2-N22 HALT, even though the surface effect ("process stops") looks similar. The mapping question matters because TRACE_MATRIX_v1 needed to decide where to attach this code path in the constitutional flowcharts.
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:20:| Triggered by | constitutional condition (∏p=0 cumulative, max-tx exhausted, OmegaAccepted, wall-clock cap, compute-cap, error-halt) | manifest hash mismatch in genesis_payload.toml |
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:28:## Closer constitutional match: FC3-E14 (`error → re-init → boot`)
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:30:FC3 has a top-level edge `init → error → re-init → boot` (line 711 of constitution.md, FC3-E13/E14). The semantics:
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:36:Trust Root verification *is* an init-time precondition check. If it fails, the constitutional response is "re-init" — return to boot. In v4, "return to boot" = the process exits and the surrounding harness (shell, batch runner) restarts it. The `panic!` is the immediate-abort leaf of FC3-E14.
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:45:2. Adding the variant would lie about the constitutional structure — we would be claiming Trust Root verification is part of the FC2 boot/tick cycle, when it is in fact a precondition for FC2 to begin.
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:50:**None now**. This OBS file documents the decision. No constitution change requested.
handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:59:- `constitution.md` line 670-714 (FC3 mermaid block) — source flowchart

handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:66:- `WalletTool::save_to_disk` — `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs:83` — cross-problem wallet persistence is not part of the constitutional state-machine diagrams.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:6:> **2026-04-25 amendment** (post-constitution V.3 修订日志, mid-session): the constitution renamed **JudgeAI → Veto-AI** (Art. V.1.3 + FC3 mermaid `judgeAI` → `vetoAI`). All TRACE_MATRIX v0 references to `JudgeAI` / `judgeAI` (rows FC3-N32, FC3-N42, FC3-N43, FC3-E4/E5/E15, edge `FC3-Veto`) should be read forward-compatibly as Veto-AI / vetoAI. v0 + `FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md` are immutable audit-trail baselines and are NOT backfilled. Constitutional clarifications also added at V.1.1 (sudo scope = constitution.md only) + V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files); these reframe how Trust Root is *enforced* (Veto-AI proposal gate + Boot manifest runtime gate) without changing what's *in* the manifest.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:19:| FC3-N34 (`readonly guard on {constitution, logs}`) | 📅 Phase 11+: add FS-level readonly check at init | ✅ implemented | B7 ships `turingosv4::boot::verify_trust_root` (`src/boot.rs:62`) — SHA-256 manifest verification at Boot. `src/main.rs` calls it as the first action and panics with `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED` on mismatch. Mechanism = content-hash check rather than FS chmod, but the constitutional intent (readonly base cannot be silently mutated between runs) is honored. |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:45:The constitutional FC3-S3 `readonly` subgraph contains FC3-N3 (`constitution as ground truth`) and FC3-N4 (`logs archive as ground truth`). PREREG § 1.8 (round-4 dual-audit PASS/PASS) extended this base for the PPUT-CCL experiment. Each addition is a research-protocol orphan with explicit constitutional justification (case-law / measurement-fidelity / pre-registration commitment).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:50:| `src/wal.rs` | FC3-N11 (`log`) implementation — append-only WAL is the constitutional logs-archive surface |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:61:| `constitution.md` | FC3-N3 (constitution as ground truth) — direct |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:62:| `cases/MANIFEST.sha256` | case-law glob hashed once into Trust Root; case law is constitutional precedent (CLAUDE.md "Common Law"), so this is FC3-N3 extension via secondary manifest |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:93:- TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED fires *before* any kernel/bus construction. The process aborts with no `Tape`, no `Bus`, no agent — there is no constitutional execution to halt.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:94:- Closer match: FC3-E14 (`init → error → re-init → boot`). Boot-panic is the immediate-abort variant; the surrounding harness (batch runner, supervisord, shell wrapper) is the "re-init" actor.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:96:See `OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md` for the rationale to keep this as panic rather than promoting it into HaltReason. No constitution change requested.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:105:- New orphan rows: **15 readonly extension paths** (above § 3) — each with constitutional justification, none requiring constitution change
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:119:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.
handover/alignment/STAGE5_VALIDATION_REPORT_2026-04-22.md:65:Relaunch **Phase 9.A seed 74677 N=50 n8** on this aligned binary. Full paper-quality baseline run. Expected ~3-5 hours wallclock. Stage 5 validation confirms the binary is aligned with the constitution; 9.A generates the data needed for Paper 1.

handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:14:So strictly only one properly-fenced `mermaid` block exists. FC-2 and FC-3 are indented pseudo-blocks with only closing backticks. Mermaid won't render them on GitHub without a proper opener. This is a constitutional-document hygiene issue that should be flagged to the human architect (Art. V.2 — the constitution is the ground truth and must be machine-parseable).
handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:230:| FC3-N3 | doc `shape: doc` classDef white | `constitution as ground truth` | readonly (inside InitAI) |
handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:245:| FC3-E1 | human | constitution | arrow `-->` | `maintain` |
handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:259:| FC3-E15 | constitution | judgeAI | arrow `-->` (multi-target: `constitution -->\|abide\| judgeAI & architectAI`) | `abide` |
handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:260:| FC3-E16 | constitution | architectAI | arrow `-->` (multi-target: `constitution -->\|abide\| judgeAI & architectAI`) | `abide` |
handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:274:None. FC-3 uses only English/natural-language labels (`maintain`, `manage`, `use`, `archive`, `init/iterate`, `init`, `make/improve`, `write`, `feedback`, `re-init`, `abide`, `veto`, `need to improve?`, `constitution as ground truth`, `logs archive as ground truth`, `Q`). No LaTeX math.
handover/alignment/FC_ELEMENTS_2026-04-22.md:302:1. **Fencing bug in constitution.md** — only FC-1 has a proper ` ```mermaid ` opener at line 325. FC-2 (line 441, `flowchart TD`) and FC-3 (line 670, `graph TB`) are indented-code pseudo-blocks with only a closing ` ``` ` (lines 530 / 714). Neither will render as mermaid on GitHub / Notion in current form. Treat as constitutional hygiene issue (Art. V.2 — ground truth must be machine-parseable).

V.3 amendment log with date / trigger / section / summary, (3) commit must trigger genesis_payload.toml constitution.md SHA-256 recompute. ArchitectAI does NOT have authority here — see Art. V.1.2 for the (non-constitution) commit-authority scope."

rules/active/R-015_trace_matrix_pub_symbol.yaml:13:message: "REMINDER (R-015 / Alignment Standard): edit touches a pub symbol in a Rust file. New OR modified pub items MUST carry `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC?-N?: <role>` doc-comment AND have an entry in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v?.md (current: v1_2026-04-25). If this is genuinely orphan, file under TRACE_MATRIX § 3 with explicit Constitutional Justification (cases/Cxxx or PREREG-§n.m). Untraced pub symbols cause silent constitutional drift."

.claude/worktrees/agent-a89c20c4/handover/ai-direct/CHECKPOINT_PHASE_2_2026-04-20.md:98:(a) is the cheapest test; (b) is the most constitutional. My recommendation is to **skip to Phase 4 next**. Phase 4 makes `append` rewards compound across problems, so the first agent to earn from a successful tape-path solve discovers it by balance growth on the NEXT problem. That's the real Hayek loop.

.claude/worktrees/agent-ae34e1cb/incidents/V-001_fund_agent_coin_printing/root_cause.md:21:Generator = Evaluator: the same AI that implemented fund_agent was auditing it, creating a structural inability to flag its own constitutional violations.

.claude/worktrees/agent-ae34e1cb/incidents/V-002_redistribute_pool_rebirth/root_cause.md:12:   - Rule 19: No post-genesis minting. The 10K coins come from nowhere. Market health cannot override conservation law — it's a constitutional constraint, not a guideline.
.claude/worktrees/agent-ae34e1cb/incidents/V-002_redistribute_pool_rebirth/root_cause.md:18:   - Scope creep avoidance — the fix was "remove fund_agent." The lesson: constitutional violations require blast-radius analysis, not point fixes.

.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:40:The constitutional requirement is explicit: Art. V.1 demands internal three-power separation, with ArchitectAI proposing changes and JudgeAI vetoing unconstitutional ones, and the constitution stored on a readonly substrate (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:562-645`). The repo does not implement that inside the Rust runtime or CI. The two Claude sub-agent files are just prompt definitions with read-only instructions (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/agents/proposer.md:1-46`, `/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/agents/auditor.md:1-19,61-62`), and the roadmap still describes P1-4 as future work because code “没有 JudgeAI 实体” (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/ROADMAP_LAUNCH_2026-04-21.md:22,82-84,107`).
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:64:Art. V.1.1 says the constitution must live on a readonly filesystem and only the human architect should hold sudo to change it (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:643-645`). The actual repo state is weaker:
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:66:- Audit-time file mode was `-rw-r--r--` (`0644`) on `/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md`.
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:67:- `core.hooksPath` was unset, and `.git/hooks/` contained only stock `*.sample` files, no active constitution-protecting hook.
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:68:- I found no CI workflow in this repo and no blocking routine that prevents constitution changes. The only routine touching the constitution is advisory and explicitly non-blocking (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/routines/daily_drift.yaml:2,15-17,64-66`).
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:70:That makes Claude's protection complaint correct: the current state is unconstitutional relative to Art. V.1.1.
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:97:- `/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/EXT_AUDIT_2026-04-21/codex_constitutional_correctness.md:72,74`
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:101:The constitution explicitly requires a background gardener agent that periodically scans and shields stale code/documentation (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:247-249`). The code does not implement that. Tape storage is explicitly append-only and “NEVER modified or removed” (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/ledger.rs:27-35`), and the grep results only surface roadmap prose about a future tombstone design (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/ROADMAP_LAUNCH_2026-04-21.md:55,152,215`). Claude was right here: the gardener is missing.
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:107:That is a deliberate Art. II.1 design choice, not an accidental bug: rejection reasons are abstracted into bounded classes before broadcast (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:439-489`), which is aligned with C-022's demand to cut off raw error contamination (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-022_context_poisoning.yaml:11-17`). But the tension Claude flagged is real, because Art. III.3 separately requires shielding horizontal correlations (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:269-279`). I agree a new precedent like C-047 would be useful to formally resolve “global abstract broadcast” versus “cross-agent decorrelation.”
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:111:- **Oneshot and hybrid still bypass Art. IV/C-043 runtime topology.** The `oneshot` condition verifies directly with `oracle.verify_omega` and returns a `PputResult` without any bus write or settlement (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:108-143,196-215`). That contradicts Art. IV's `∏p=1 -> wtool -> Q_{t+1}` rule (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:521-524`) and C-043's mandatory `append_oracle_accepted` requirement (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-043_mandatory_wtool_on_omega.yaml:15-20,26-34`). Claude's internal audit treated topology as effectively landed, but the legacy oneshot path remains constitutionally divergent.
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:121:The branch should not be reverted outright: the core Phase 7 runtime work, WAL persistence, artifact persistence, and partial-proof oracle behavior are real and materially advanced. But it also should not be treated as constitutionally clean enough to merge-forward without follow-up. The unresolved issues are not cosmetic: Art. V is still only thin/manual governance, the constitution file is not physically protected, the blessed-write path is capability-weak, the legacy oneshot path still bypasses mandatory `wtool`, and the agent prompt appears to surface incorrect wallet state. Those are merge blockers for an external constitutional-correctness standard.

.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:5:**Core thesis (2026-04-22 revision post Codex CHALLENGE)**: **First implementation of a Turing-machine-topology constitutional substrate for LLM swarms, with cryptographic capability tokens and runtime-verified three-process governance. We empirically test N Hayek-inspired market mechanisms on this substrate and report observed effects on PPUT (Progress Per Unit Time).**
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:18:constitutional-layer gap: mechanisms that are agent-visible at the prompt
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:39:- LLM agents can drift from constitutional intent under adversarial pressure or via prompt-level constraint erosion
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:44:1. **Constitutional microkernel**: Rust implementation where Art. I-V of a natural-language constitution compile into specific Rust invariants (enumerated in § 3)
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:59:- **Koolen/Olshausen constitutional architecture**: top-level governance as immutable axioms (our § 3.1 mirror)
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:73:- Self-referential: constitution encodes its own modification permissions
.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_OUTLINE_2026-04-22.md:148:- Runtime-verified constitutional compliance is novel for LLM agent systems

.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/handover/ai-direct/ROADMAP_LAUNCH_2026-04-21.md:247:某大团队靠算力优势拿走 50%+ 的 reputation，之后任何 constitution 建议都是他们主导。**缓解**: reputation ↔ 治理投票权解耦。宪法修订**仍是研究者 sudo** (Art. V.2)，即便上线后也不开放给 reputation holders。把"代码治理"和"宪法治理"分两层，只开放前者。

tests/harness_validation.sh:30:# T-002: constitution.md exists

tests/harness_validation.sh:34:[ -f "$ROOT/constitution.md" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/handover/bible.md" ] && pass "T-003: constitution.md is sole alignment doc" || fail "T-003: constitution.md missing or bible.md still exists" ""
tests/harness_validation.sh:49:RULE_COUNT=$(find "$ROOT/rules/active" -name "*.yaml" | wc -l)
tests/harness_validation.sh:50:[ "$RULE_COUNT" -eq 10 ] && pass "T-007: 10 active rules" || fail "T-007: Expected 10 rules" "found $RULE_COUNT"
tests/harness_validation.sh:87:RULES="$ROOT/rules/active"
tests/harness_validation.sh:88:TRACES="/tmp/v4_harness_test_traces"
tests/harness_validation.sh:92:OUTPUT=$(echo "normal safe code" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/bus.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)

tests/harness_validation.sh:102:OUTPUT=$(echo "fund_agent(agent, 1000)" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/bus.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
tests/harness_validation.sh:107:OUTPUT=$(echo "std::fs::remove_file(wal_path)" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "src/wal.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
tests/harness_validation.sh:118:OUTPUT=$(echo "lean tactic theorem proof" | python3 "$ENGINE" --file "experiments/test/src/skill.rs" --rules-dir "$RULES" --log /dev/null --traces-dir "$TRACES" 2>&1)
tests/harness_validation.sh:148:echo "--- 3. Hook Simulation (judge.sh via stdin JSON) ---"

tests/harness_validation.sh:158:# T-023: judge.sh blocks git push --force
tests/harness_validation.sh:161:[ $EC -eq 2 ] && pass "T-023: judge.sh blocks git push --force" || fail "T-023: Should block force push" "exit $EC"
tests/harness_validation.sh:163:# T-024: judge.sh blocks git reset --hard
tests/harness_validation.sh:166:[ $EC -eq 2 ] && pass "T-024: judge.sh blocks git reset --hard" || fail "T-024: Should block hard reset" "exit $EC"
tests/harness_validation.sh:168:# T-025: judge.sh allows safe bash
tests/harness_validation.sh:171:[ $EC -eq 0 ] && pass "T-025: judge.sh allows safe bash (ls)" || fail "T-025: Safe bash should pass" "exit $EC"
tests/harness_validation.sh:173:# T-026: judge.sh blocks WAL deletion via bash
tests/harness_validation.sh:176:[ $EC -eq 2 ] && pass "T-026: judge.sh blocks WAL file deletion" || fail "T-026: Should block .wal deletion" "exit $EC"
tests/harness_validation.sh:178:# T-027: judge.sh blocks sed on kernel.rs

codex
I audited actual `HEAD` (`e94e1b9`), not just the pasted excerpts. Focused verification passes: `cargo test --test fc_alignment_conformance` = 17 passed / 9 ignored, `boot::tests::verify_trust_root_passes_on_intact_repo` passed, and the A0 Trust Root presence test passed.

**Findings**

1. **R-018 is not airtight.** The hook handles `Bash` first and exits before any `constitution.md` file guard, so `python/perl/tee/sed -i constitution.md` style mutations are not blocked by R-018 ([judge.sh:19](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:19), [judge.sh:57](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:57)). The Edit/Write guard also requires nonempty `CONTENT`, so an empty replacement/delete edit can bypass the special case and fall through to the `*.md` skip-list ([judge.sh:16](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:16), [judge.sh:66](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:66), [judge.sh:87](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:87)). This contradicts C-071’s “不可绕过” claim ([C-071:63](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-071_constitution_amendment_process.yaml:63)).

2. **R-018 does not implement the documented sudo process.** C-071 says R-018 greps the verbatim chat phrase and checks V.3 entry discipline ([C-071:44](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-071_constitution_amendment_process.yaml:44), [C-071:48](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-071_constitution_amendment_process.yaml:48)), but the rule is `pattern: ".*"` over file content and unconditional `block` ([R-018:8](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/active/R-018_constitution_amendment_sudo.yaml:8), [R-018:12](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/active/R-018_constitution_amendment_sudo.yaml:12)). That is safe as a hard stop, but it is not the claimed authorized-amendment workflow.

3. **Model canonicalization is incomplete and already violated.** PREREG pins `deepseek-v4-flash` and says both `deepseek-chat` and `deepseek-reasoner` are deprecated aliases ([PREREG:705](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:705), [PREREG:706](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:706)). R-019 only warns on `deepseek-chat` ([R-019:9](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/active/R-019_model_snapshot_canonical.yaml:9)), while the production evaluator defaults to `deepseek-reasoner` ([evaluator.rs:203](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:203)) and the Trust Root calibration runner defaults to `deepseek-chat` ([run_p0_calibration.sh:43](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:43)). `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` then inherits that alias if unset ([evaluator.rs:1216](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1216)).

4. **The cross-crate Lean oracle witness is falsely claimed but absent.** The ignored root stub says it is “covered in `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs`” ([fc_alignment_conformance.rs:262](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs:262)), but that file does not exist. The symbol is real and public ([lean4_oracle.rs:28](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:28), [lean4_oracle.rs:98](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:98), [lean4_oracle.rs:112](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:112)); A0 should add the sub-crate conformance test before Phase A.

5. **A0 rollback protection is only hash-level, not semantic.** Trust Root now hashes `rules/MANIFEST.sha256`, `rules/engine.py`, `judge.sh`, and root conformance tests ([genesis_payload.toml:85](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/genesis_payload.toml:85)), and boot catches unmanifested tampering ([boot.rs:71](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/boot.rs:71), [boot.rs:85](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/boot.rs:85)). But the semantic test that asserts A0 entries exist lives in `trust_root_immutability.rs` ([trust_root_immutability.rs:111](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/trust_root_immutability.rs:111)) and is not itself in Trust Root. Also the older harness validation script is stale: it still expects 10 rules and 35 cases ([harness_validation.sh:48](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/harness_validation.sh:48), [harness_validation.sh:60](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/harness_validation.sh:60)).

R-014 should be scope-narrowed and its message is stale: it still says 20 entries while v2 says 24 ([R-014:13](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/active/R-014_trust_root_manifest_drift.yaml:13), [TRACE v2:61](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:61)).

V.1.2’s “any non-constitution file” language ([constitution.md:606](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:606)). C-074 duplication with memory is acceptable. Do not put all tests in Trust Root, but do add the Trust Root immutability test if it is the rollback guard.

1. **R-018 is not airtight.** The hook handles `Bash` first and exits before any `constitution.md` file guard, so `python/perl/tee/sed -i constitution.md` style mutations are not blocked by R-018 ([judge.sh:19](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:19), [judge.sh:57](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:57)). The Edit/Write guard also requires nonempty `CONTENT`, so an empty replacement/delete edit can bypass the special case and fall through to the `*.md` skip-list ([judge.sh:16](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:16), [judge.sh:66](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:66), [judge.sh:87](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:87)). This contradicts C-071’s “不可绕过” claim ([C-071:63](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-071_constitution_amendment_process.yaml:63)).
